title: 'smallhold init' CLI command
description: Implement the init subcommand that bootstraps a new instance: creates the data directory, generates a random secret_key (64 hex chars), writes a config.toml skeleton, and initializes the SQLite database with the full schema. This is the entry point for any new deployment.
design: Generate secret_key with rand crate (32 random bytes, hex-encoded). Write config.toml template with all sections. Run migrations via sqlx::migrate\!().run(&pool). Print: 'Instance initialized. Next: edit config.toml, set admin password with smallhold admin set-password, create a persona with smallhold persona create <username>'
acceptance_criteria: smallhold init creates config.toml with correct sections and a generated secret_key; creates the database file at the configured path; runs migrations; prints clear next-steps instructions; fails gracefully if config.toml already exists (prompt to confirm overwrite or use --force)
